back_translation: Now at the end of twenty years, during which Solomon had built the house of the LORD and his own palace,
Solomon rebuilt the cities that Hiram had given him, and settled the Israelites there.
Solomon went to Hamath-zobah and captured it.
He built Tadmor in the wilderness, and all the store cities that he built in Hamath.
He rebuilt Upper Beth-horon and Lower Beth-horon as fortified cities, with walls, gates, and bars,

and Baalath, and all the store cities that belonged to Solomon, and all the chariot cities and the cavalry cities, and whatever he desired to build in Jerusalem, in Lebanon, and throughout all the land of his dominion.
As for all the people who remained of the Hittites, Amorites, Perizzites, Hivites, and Jebusites, who were not Israelites—
their descendants who remained in the land, whom the Israelites had not destroyed—Solomon conscripted them as slave labor, as they are to this day.
But Solomon did not make any of the Israelites slaves for his work, because they were his soldiers, the chiefs of his officers, and the commanders of his chariots and cavalry.
These were the chief officers of King Solomon: two hundred fifty who supervised the people.

Solomon brought the daughter of Pharaoh up from the City of David to the palace he had built for her, for he said, “My wife shall not dwell in the palace of David king of Israel, because the places to which the ark of the LORD has come are holy.”
Then Solomon offered burnt offerings to the LORD on the altar of the LORD that he had built in front of the portico,

as the daily duty required, offering according to the commandment of Moses for the Sabbaths, the New Moons, and the three annual feasts—the Feast of Unleavened Bread, the Feast of Weeks, and the Feast of Tabernacles.

He appointed, according to the ordinance of David his father, the divisions of the priests for their service, and the Levites for their duties to praise and minister with the priests as each day required, and the gatekeepers by their divisions at each gate; for so David the man of God had commanded.
They did not turn aside from the king's command concerning the priests and the Levites in any matter, including the treasuries.
Thus all the work of Solomon was accomplished, from the day the foundation of the house of the LORD was laid until it was finished. So the house of the LORD was completed.
Then Solomon went to Ezion-geber and to Eloth, on the seacoast in the land of Edom.

And Hiram sent him ships by the hands of his servants, sailors skilled with the sea; they went with Solomon's servants to Ophir and brought from there four hundred fifty talents of gold and delivered it to King Solomon.
